verse_text: And those who stood by said, 'Do you revile God's high priest?'

links.context: a-1r These verses occur only in the Greek LXX manuscripts and appear there as an addition to the Book of Esther. 1:1d-1j The dream (which is interpreted in 10:1-3k) prepares Mordecai for the test he will face and reveals that God is behind the course of human events. 1:1m-1r This plot, recounted in 2:21-23, serves to describe the animosity between Haman and Mordecai. 1:1-6 Artaxerxes is imperfect, passionate, and self-indulgent. Nevertheless, by virtue of his office and by reigning over many nations, he functions as a figure for God. The dignity of an earthly office is not diminished by the failings of the person holding it (see [[JOH.11:51]]; [[ACT.23:1]]-5).
